text.title: Service Mesh Decision Tree
text.summary: Routing tree from observable signal to a rule id in `01-core-rules.xml`. Every leaf resolves to either a rule id or `skip-this-methodology`.

decision-tree.root-question: Which observable signal best describes the current state of the artefact under review?

branch.question: How many services share traffic in the cluster?

conclusion.verdict: <5 services → direct mTLS via cert-manager is simpler than a mesh

branch.question: Is sub-1ms latency a hard requirement?

conclusion.verdict: any proxy layer adds latency; resolve without a mesh

branch.question: Is the requirement mTLS-only with no L7 advanced features?

conclusion.verdict: use Istio Ambient (ztunnel L4) — lowest overhead

branch.question: Are you about to switch on STRICT mTLS without permissive validation?

conclusion.verdict: stage permissive for ≥7 days, document exceptions, then promote namespace-by-namespace

branch.question: Is mesh config being applied via `kubectl apply` outside git?

conclusion.verdict: move to GitOps (Argo CD / Flux); reject manual applies in production

branch.question: Has outlier detection been omitted from new VirtualDestinations?

conclusion.verdict: add outlier detection with 30s ejection on every service before going live
signal does not match; route to a different methodology or proceed without this one

section.title: Usage
section.p: Apply the tree top-to-bottom against the artefact (PR diff, config, design doc). The first matching branch is decisive; later branches are not evaluated. If multiple branches match, fix in the order listed — they typically depend on earlier fixes.
